title: VTAM / Tactile-WAM: Predictive Contact Intelligence
description: Ongoing DaiMeng VTAM proposal: build predictive contact intelligence for dexterous manipulation, moving from reactive tactile feedback to proactive manipulation with future tactile/contact latent prediction and action decoding.
summary: VTAM is now an ongoing DaiMeng robot research proposal: “From Reactive Manipulation to Proactive Manipulation.” The technical core is a Vision-Tactile-Action World Model where future tactile/contact latents act as an Internal Contact Model. The updated proposal positions high-efficiency VTAM as a Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model: visual context is cached at low frequency, tactile/contact latents and action tokens roll out quickly, and heavy video/tactile decoders are called only for supervision, visualization, or key planning branches. The project should be evaluated by contact timing, slip/contact risk, latency/Hz/VRAM, actual-vs-zero/wrong-action gates, task success, disturbance recovery, and safety force limits.

subprojects.label: A
subprojects.title: Universal tactile encoder
subprojects.body: Learn a transferable tactile latent over marker flow, tactile images, and multi-finger contact sequences, preserving contact geometry, force/slip cues, and task-relevant dynamics.

subprojects.label: B
subprojects.title: Temporal Action-Token VTAM
subprojects.body: Use visual tokens, tactile/contact tokens, temporal action tokens, and shared-world tokens to predict future contact/tactile latent trajectories and action latents.

subprojects.label: C
subprojects.title: Fast contact/action rollout
subprojects.body: Avoid full video generation at every step; cache visual/layer latents, run sparse compute around contact events, and distill a fast student from a heavy teacher.

subprojects.label: D
subprojects.title: System-0 / System-1 control
subprojects.body: Run System-1 at low frequency for future contact prediction and action chunks, and System-0 at high frequency for tactile-latent residual corrections.

asset_alt: Tactile-WAM pipeline from human retargeted robot video to tactile prediction and world action model output
asset_caption: VTAM proposal: universal tactile encoding feeds a Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model; fast contact/action rollout and System-0 tactile servo turn predicted future contact into executable manipulation.
details: Origin: this was discussed with the Wuji team on 2026-06-11 as a Tactile-WAM proposal. The key shift is to stop treating tactile as only another observation channel; tactile should become the contact-grounding signal that turns video imagination into action-capable prediction.

Demo 3 first: use an external camera to observe human hand / arm motion, retarget that motion into the video model, and generate a robot arm + dexterous hand performing the same interaction. This should be a video-model demo, not necessarily immediate real-robot control.

Demo 1 second: from the generated robot video in the previous step, estimate tactile output: contact timing, contact patch, pressure / force proxy, slip risk, and contact-state transitions. This is the tactile world model layer.

Final target: extend the predictive world model into WAM. Instead of only predicting future video or tactile maps under given actions, the model should produce the action needed to realize the imagined contact-rich future.

Why this is not just VTLA: VTLA / Tactile Intuition remains the broad survey for tactile representation and benchmark choice. Tactile-WAM is a separate Survey card for the concrete proposal chain: generated robot video -> predicted tactile -> action-generating model.

Why this relates to Tri-View / Image-Layered IDM: the robot arm/hand video generator and layer decomposition provide the visual state backbone. Tactile-WAM adds a contact-state head and then an action head, especially for tasks where visual evidence alone cannot determine contact.

Why this relates to DexGello: DexGello can provide aligned real data for arm/hand/finger state, while Wuji tactile / glove streams can provide tactile supervision. Tactile-WAM can use that data to train video-to-touch and touch-to-action components.

Good first benchmark families: pressing, wiping, grasping, in-hand adjustment, cutting/peeling, and assembly/insertion. Choose tasks where tactile is genuinely necessary: occlusion, slip, force threshold, contact/no-contact ambiguity, or object deformation.

DaiMeng proposal update 2026-06-27: promote this card from Survey to ongoing Research because VTAM is now a live proposal/project track. The dashboard framing should use VTAM as the proposal name and Tactile-WAM as the technical mechanism: video-to-touch prediction plus action decoding for contact-rich manipulation.

Ongoing execution scope: build data alignment first (external/RGB-D video, wrist/hand video, Wuji qpos/tactile streams, robot state/action, timestamps, calibration snapshot), then run the three-stage demo: retargeted robot video generation, video-to-tactile/contact prediction, and action-head / WAM decoding.

DaiMeng proposal extraction 2026-06-27: the updated 2026-06-26 proposal is titled “面向灵巧操作的预测式接触智能研究 / From Reactive Manipulation to Proactive Manipulation / 基于 Vision-Tactile-Action World Model 的主动接触操作框架.” It reframes VTAM as predictive contact intelligence: future tactile latent is the Internal Contact Model, not an auxiliary sensor feature.

Proposal technical core 2026-06-27: high-efficiency VTAM should be a Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model, not generic vision/tactile feature concatenation or a plain video-model finetune. Token streams: visual tokens, tactile/contact tokens, temporal action tokens, and shared-world tokens. The model predicts future tactile/contact latent trajectories, contact risk, action latent/action chunks, and optional video rollout.

Proposal acceleration path 2026-06-27: use a fast contact/action path. Visual features or robot/object/background layer latents are cached at low frequency; tactile/contact latents and temporal action tokens roll out at higher frequency; heavy video/high-dimensional tactile decoders are used only for training supervision, visualization, or key planning branches. Add contact-triggered sparse compute and teacher-student distillation.

Proposal control architecture 2026-06-27: System-1 is the low-frequency contact world model, planned at roughly 5-20 Hz, predicting future tactile/contact state, risk, and action chunks. System-0 is a high-frequency tactile servo, roughly 50-100 Hz or higher, comparing observed tactile latent with predicted target tactile latent and outputting residual corrections such as finger micro-motion, normal-force adjustment, tangential slip compensation, or insertion alignment.

Proposal prior-work chain 2026-06-27: OmniViTac establishes large-scale vision-tactile-action data and tactile locality/contact-triggered value; OmniVTA shows future touch as predictive contact representation with TactileVAE and visuo-tactile world modeling; TacForeSight shows future touch as action guidance through force-conditioned tactile latent prediction and predictive tactile-conditioned policy. VTAM is the next step: an internal contact model plus active control loop.

Proposal evaluation update 2026-06-27: evaluation must go beyond task success and visual plausibility. Required metrics include tactile prediction error, contact timing error, slip/contact risk error, actual/zero/wrong-action gate, latency per step, control Hz, VRAM, teacher-student error, disturbance recovery time, slip count, safe force thresholds, action smoothness, and cross-object/position/tool generalization.

references.title: User-shared XHS demo reference 1
references.notes: User-provided visual demo reference from the Wuji proposal discussion. Use as the inspiration for estimating tactile signals from generated robot video; content may require Xiaohongshu app/login for full viewing.

references.title: User-shared XHS demo reference 2
references.notes: User-provided supporting demo reference from the Wuji proposal discussion; keep as a pointer pending manual viewing in the app.

references.title: User-shared WeChat Channels demo reference
references.notes: User-provided demo reference for the external-camera human-hand retargeting idea: use human motion to control a video model of a robot arm plus dexterous hand.

references.title: Visuo-Tactile World Models
references.notes: Closest world-model reference: vision and tactile latents are predicted autoregressively under action conditioning, and the model is used for planning in imagination.

references.title: OmniVTA: Visuo-Tactile World Modeling for Contact-Rich Robotic Manipulation
references.notes: Reference for large-scale visuo-tactile-action data, tactile prediction, contact-aware fusion, and high-frequency tactile correction in contact-rich manipulation.

references.title: TouchAnything: Bimanual Tactile Estimation from Egocentric Video
references.notes: Reference for video-to-touch prediction: synchronized egocentric/wrist videos, bimanual hand pose, and dense pressure maps; supports the Tactile-WAM tactile-from-video head.

references.title: World Action Models: The Next Frontier in Embodied AI
references.notes: Definition reference for WAMs: unify predictive state modeling with action generation, rather than learning only reactive observation-to-action mappings.

references.title: World Action Models are Zero-shot Policies
references.notes: Reference for joint video+action modeling as a policy route. Tactile-WAM extends this logic with tactile/contact prediction for dexterous manipulation.

references.title: 1X World Model: From Video to Action
references.notes: Industry reference for deriving robot actions from text-conditioned video generation; useful framing for video-model-to-action conversion.

references.title: DaiMeng VTAM proposal update, 2026-06-26
references.notes: Local DOCX extracted on 2026-06-27. Key additions: Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model, fast contact/action path, System-0/System-1 active contact control, and explicit latency/action-gate/contact-risk evaluation.

risks_decisions: Do not pitch this as only tactile prediction. The project needs the full chain: human-retargeted robot video generation, tactile estimation, and action output.
Synthetic tactile risk: tactile predicted from generated video can become visually plausible but physically wrong. Ground it with real Wuji / tactile sensor streams whenever possible.

Action-decoding risk: a WAM head must be evaluated on executable actions, not only video fidelity. The acceptance metric should include action plausibility, robot limits, contact success, and rollout consistency.
Data-alignment risk: tactile is high-frequency and local, while video is lower-frequency and global. The logger must preserve timestamps, calibration, contact frames, and sensor metadata.
Novelty boundary: VT-WM and OmniVTA already cover visuo-tactile world models. The differentiator should be external-camera retargeted robot-video generation plus tactile-grounded WAM action output.

Status decision 2026-06-27: move VTAM / Tactile-WAM from Survey to ongoing Research. It should now be tracked as an active DaiMeng/Wuji proposal project rather than only a tactile representation idea.

Proposal decision 2026-06-27: dashboard should track this as VTAM / Predictive Contact Intelligence, with Tactile-WAM as the video-to-touch-to-action path. The contribution claim is proactive contact manipulation through future tactile/contact latent prediction and fast action rollout, not only tactile sensing or multimodal fusion.

Architecture boundary 2026-06-27: avoid a video-first architecture where every control step decodes full future frames. Online control should use low-dimensional contact/action latents; video rollout is optional for training, visualization, and audit.

Acceptance boundary 2026-06-27: do not accept demos based only on pretty video or qualitative tactile maps. A passing VTAM demo must separate actual actions from zero/wrong actions, predict contact timing/slip risk, show latency/Hz/VRAM numbers, and improve at least one contact-rich manipulation task under perturbation.

timeline.target_note: Execute the proposal roadmap: tactile encoder -> Temporal Action-Token VTAM -> fast contact/action rollout -> System-0/System-1 control -> contact-rich evaluation.

milestones.label: Phase 0
milestones.text: Offline VTAM / Tactile-WAM data path: external or robot video, tactile/contact labels, action schema, synchronization error, contact timing, baseline predictor.

milestones.label: Phase 1
milestones.text: Temporal Action-Token Shared-World Model with action/contact/tactile tokenization and held-out actual/zero/wrong-action gates.

milestones.label: Phase 2
milestones.text: Fast student versus heavy teacher; report latency, VRAM, speedup, and precision loss.

milestones.label: Phase 3
milestones.text: System-0 high-frequency tactile servo driven by predicted-vs-observed tactile latent error; evaluate task success, slip count, disturbance recovery, and safety force thresholds.
